Empires & Puzzles Goopy Review: The 3-Star Mana Control Monster

Goopy might look like a harmless blob of jelly, but this Morlovia 3-star packs a serious punch. With AoE damage and crippling mana generation debuffs, he is a Rush Tournament nightmare. Find out why this slimeball deserves a spot on your roster.
Goopy: The Sticky Situation Your Opponents Can't Escape
Goopy, The Gelatinous Goo: Sugar Rush and Mana Crush

Goopy is a Rare (3-star) Nature hero from the Return to Morlovia (Halloween) event. Released back in 2022, this sticky little fellow quickly established himself as a nuisance in the 3-star arena. While he might look like a sugary treat gone wrong, his ability to manipulate the board state and punish enemies based on tile presence makes him a tactical asset. He operates as an Average speed damage dealer who specializes in slowing down the opposition, essentially functioning as a "mini" version of some larger mana-control heavy hitters.

Visually, Goopy is a delight for anyone who appreciates the grosser side of Halloween. He is essentially a large, green, translucent blob of slime—very reminiscent of a certain ghost-busting franchise's hungry specter—clutching a lollipop and overflowing from a cauldron. The art captures a playful yet grotesque vibe, with candy floating inside his gelatinous body. It is a colorful and distinct design that stands out among the usual knights and sorcerers.

When we look at Goopy's stats in the context of the modern 3-star meta, he holds up reasonably well but shows some age. His base Attack of 500 is respectable, allowing him to punch through defenses in Rare tournaments. His Health (808) is solid, but his Defense (467) is on the lower side, making him somewhat squishy if focused down. Compared to the latest power-crept 3-stars like the Construct or Astral Elf heroes, his stats are average, but his special skill utility often outweighs the raw stat deficit.
Goopy's special skill, "Sugar Rush," is a dynamic ability that scales with the board.
He starts with a base 125% damage to all enemies. While this seems low initially, it can scale up to 221% depending on the number of Nature shields on the board.
This is the crown jewel of his kit. He applies a base -24% mana generation debuff to all enemies. Like the damage, this scales with Nature shields, capping at a debilitating -40%.

This scaling mechanic means Goopy rewards you heavily for playing a mono-Green or Green-heavy team, or simply for having a good board. Even at the base level, a -24% mana slow for 3 turns is significant in 3-star tournaments where dispels can be scarce. At -40%, it is practically a mana freeze.
The Morlovia family bonus provides extra mana generation (up to +12% with 3 heroes).
Barbarian. The emblem node gives a small mana boost, but typically Barbarians don't get a massive mana break.

In Rush Attack tournaments (Very Fast speed), Goopy becomes a monster. Outside of Rush, he needs 10 tiles to charge. With the family bonus and a 3-star troop, you can shave this down slightly, but he remains firmly in the Average bracket.

(221 - 125) / 12 = 8 Nature shields.
Finding 8 shields on the board is feasible, especially if you are holding tiles. Hitting 5 enemies for 221% at the 3-star level is a massive amount of total damage output.

(40 - 24) / 2 = 8 Nature shields.
A -40% mana generation drop is immense. It forces the opponent to need almost double the tiles to charge their skills.
Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities
The Return to Morlovia family has seen updates over the years, giving these heroes more staying power.

The Morlovia family bonus grants mana generation. For a 3-star hero, this is excellent as it helps counteract the lack of high-level mana troops available in Rare tournaments. Pairing Goopy with other Halloween 3-stars (like Vlad or the newer additions) can make your team noticeably faster.

This passive is incredibly strong. When Goopy casts his special, he has a 65% chance to steal the newest dispellable buff from a random enemy. If there are no buffs to steal, he instead deals 150% damage to all enemies.

This is a win-win passive. Either you strip a defensive buff (like Defense Up or Counterattack) and take it for yourself, or you deal a massive chunk of extra damage. Note that the "150% damage to all" is a separate hit from his special skill, meaning if the condition is met, his total damage output skyrockets.
For a damage dealer like Goopy, this is the standard and preferred Aether Power. It boosts his attack stat at the start of the battle, making his special skill and tile damage even more potent.

When Goopy was released, he was immediately recognized as a "Rush Tournament King." His ability to slow mana for the entire enemy team at Very Fast speed (in Rush) made him a staple in top-tier defenses. He was effectively a 3-star Little John or Treevil-lite.

While his stats have fallen behind the curve of the newest event heroes, his utility remains untouched. Damage percentages have crept up in the game, but an AoE mana slow is a mechanic that does not age. The update to his passive (adding the damage alternative) kept him relevant by ensuring he does something valuable even when enemies don't have buffs.
Goopy remains a top-tier pick for
3-star tournaments and is very strong in

due to his high damage potential. In standard play, his Average speed holds him back slightly compared to the blistering speed of newer heroes, but he is far from obsolete. He is a tool you keep in your box specifically for when you need to shut down the enemy's mana generation.
At the time of this review, Goopy does not have a costume.
As a Barbarian, Goopy has access to the Bleed talent. While the bleed damage itself is minor, the attack stats on the Barbarian path are valuable.
Maximizes the damage of his special skill and the secondary damage from his passive. Since he hits all enemies, every point of Attack stat is multiplied across 5 targets.
Leaves him with lower defense, making him vulnerable to snipers.
Increases his survivability, allowing him to fire his mana-slowing special more often. Useful if you use him as a tank in Rush tournaments.
path is recommended. Goopy's primary job is to swing the momentum of the match with a big hit and a mana slow. You want that hit to hurt as much as possible.
AoE mana generation reduction is one of the most powerful abilities in the game.
Has the potential to hit very hard (221%) if the board is green-heavy.
The ability to steal buffs or deal extra damage adds a layer of versatility that many 3-stars lack.
Becomes an absolute menace in Very Fast wars and tournaments.
His ceiling is high, but his floor (125% damage, -24% mana) is just average. He needs Nature shields to be great.
Without Rush rules, he can be too slow to stop Fast snipers from killing him before he fires.
His base defense stat makes him susceptible to being one-shot by strong red hitters.
This is his playground. At Very Fast speed, he fires constantly, keeping the enemy team permanently slowed.
His damage output coupled with the passive damage makes him a strong nuker when healing is disabled.
Useful for mana control on boss waves, though his animation speed and reliance on tiles might slow down competitive runs.
Goopy shines when he is supported by heroes who can keep him alive or capitalize on his mana control.
In a Rush tournament, Faiez blocks ailments, Goopy slows the enemy, and Noril deals heavy damage. This trio is incredibly difficult to break.
Dawn cuts mana, Kvasir blocks minions (which often give mana), and Goopy slows generation. The enemy will struggle to fire a single special skill.
Heroes who can resist the mana ailment can ruin Goopy's day.
If the enemy has a cleanser charged (like Faiez or Helo), they can wipe away Goopy's mana debuff instantly.
Avoid using him in events with Green reflection (like certain levels of Contest of Elements or Challenge Festival), as he will kill himself.
If you pull Goopy, level him. He is an excellent 3-star hero who will serve you well in tournaments for a long time. Prioritize his attack stat to help you clear map stages and quests more easily.

You likely have Goopy leveled already. If not, he is worth the feeders solely for 3-star Rush tournaments. While you might have newer, flashier toys, the combination of AoE damage and mana slow is a classic utility that rarely goes out of style. He does not necessarily need a second Limit Break (Alpha Aether) unless you are extremely competitive in the 3-star arena, but a single Limit Break is a solid investment.
The community reaction to Goopy was a mix of amusement at his design and respect for his kit.
One player noted, "Don't let his sweet face fool you, this guy is a MONSTER. With 8 green shields he hits harder than Waqas."
Another famously commented, "He is the Nature Sudri," highlighting his potential dominance in Rush formats.
The "Ghostbusters" comparison was inevitable, with many players affectionately calling him "Slimer."
There was some debate about his passive changes over time, but the consensus remains that he is a "true flubbergastly monster" when the stars (and tiles) align.

Goopy is a hero that perfectly balances fun design with serious utility. He is not just a joke character; he is a legitimate threat in the 3-star meta, particularly when speed is equalized in Rush tournaments. His ability to turn a board full of green tiles into a devastating nuke and a mana freeze makes him a unique asset. While he may not be the tankiest hero, his offensive presence is undeniable. If you want to leave your opponents stuck in the mud while you secure the win, Goopy is your guy.
A comprehensive review of Goopy, the Rare Nature hero from the Return to Morlovia event. We analyze his stats, special skill, mana control capabilities, and viability in 3-star tournaments.
